query: If I take a leave of absence, am I exempt from the tuition and Students' Union fees?

queries: If I take a leave of absence, am I exempt from the tuition and Students' Union fees?

You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. However, the leave of absence does not exempt you from paying the Student's Union fee. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? A [reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
retrieved.text: fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

Depending on the semester and the reason for your application, you must meet different deadlines:

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: you have to pay the Student's Union fee every semester within the admission period, even if you are exempt from paying the tuition fee. The Student's Union (ÖH) fee does not remain at the university but is transferred to the Austrian National Union of Students. Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students federal body of Representatives](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en) - [Student’s Union of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Tolerance semesters](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31297&cHash=f0dee5ae0ea77af89fdfedaca07ca13b) - [Re-enrolment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31949&cHash=3e7137171e154715f00818454de75f06) - [Students’ Union (ÖH) insurance](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31953&cHash=ad6e71b25212dfc34b1314efc4ae3cb1) - [Payment period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32002&cHash=50174e9655aa9a11d1bb85ebc71fc50f) - Studienberechtigungsprüfung The Studienberechtigungsprüfung gives you the opportunity of admission to a degree programme if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. However, it is no general university entrance qualification. It is only valid for a certain group of degree programmes. Related Links: - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/) Related entries: - [General university entrance qualification](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31898&cHash=9a39467d6150693b5f0f6ba07ed49907) - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) The StudiesServiceCenter (in German: StudienServiceCenter, SSC) and the StudiesServiceUnit (in German: StudienServiceStelle, SSS) are central points of contact for questions about your degree programme. Each degree programme has a StudiesServiceCenter; the majority of degree programmes also have a StudiesServiceUnit, which informs you about your degree programme and the administration of your studies. On the websites of your SSC/SSS, you can find important information about your degree programme, relevant news and often forms to download. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You are not able to or do not want to pay the remaining difference? Please contact us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/706). We can remit the part of the fee you have already paid. - I am admitted to a continuing education programme at the Postgraduate Center and a bachelor/diploma or master programme. What do I have to keep in mind? If you are admitted at the Postgraduate and to a degree programme there are two options: 1. Only the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. No further payment or re-enrolment is necessary. You will be automatically re-enroled in your degree programme when the fee is received at the Postgraduate Center. 2. The tuition fee and the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you (due to your citizenship or because you have exceeded the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters) You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. In addition you have to pay the tuition fee (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
retrieved.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

After your application was approved, the same rules as for students with [citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland](#c1123190) apply to you. Keep the deadlines for your application for

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text: / - I am EU/EEA citizen. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? Check [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Study overview to see which degree programmes you are admitted to. As soon as you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna all degree programmes are continued. It does not matter if you actively study them or not. The tuition fee is prescribed if you exceed the stipulated degree programme duration plus tolerance semesters. If a degree programme that you (currently) do not want to continue is in your study overview, [you can close it](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/). The amount of the tuition fee will then be calculated anew. - I have equal status. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? If you have equal status with EU/EEA citizens you are exempt from the tuition fee as long as - your residence permit is valid and - you are within the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters. [Detailed information about equal status with EU/EEA citizens](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/) - Will my Erasmus+ semester be counted for the tuition-free period for EU/EEA citizens? Semesters during which you are abroad with a mobility programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would like to take a leave of absence. Is that possible? Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more

retrieved.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
retrieved.text: the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - Application for leave of absence granted After paying the tuition fee your [application for leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) was granted? A reimbursement is only possible if you have submitted your application for leave of absence before 30 November (for the winter semester) and 30 April (for the summer semester). - Application for remission granted Your application for remission of the tuition fee was granted after paying the tuition fee? The tuition fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too high You have paid a too high fee? The difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too little You have paid too little and are therefore no longer admitted at the University of Vienna? The fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - Reimbursement of the Students' Union fee The Students' Union fee does not remain at the University of Vienna. It is forwarded to the Students' Union. Therefore you have to submit your application for reimbursement [directly at the Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). The application is

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text: or - Have a residence permit other than "Student" (Aufenhaltsbewilligung Student). After your application was approved, the same rules as for students with [citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland](#c1123190) apply to you. Keep the deadlines for your application for equal status in mind! - Exemption 3: citizenship from the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland with admission to the degree programme before summer semester 2021 Students with this citizenship pay the same tuition fee as students with [citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland](#c1123190) if you - were admitted to the degree programme before 31 December 2020 and - do not interrupt the admission. If the degree programme is interrupted for one or more semesters, the same rules as for [students with non-EU/EEA citizenship](#c1123195) apply from the time of resumption of studies. - Exemption 4: Ukrainian citizens The University of Vienna automatically remits the tuition fee for winter semester 2026/27 for degree and non-degree students with Ukrainian citizenship. You only have to pay the Students' Union fee.

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period of the respective semester.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

retrieved.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
retrieved.text: programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the University of Vienna. You therefore are also able to take examinations from courses at the University of Vienna that took place during your semester abroad (e.g. at a later examination date). If you have to pay the tuition fee, you can apply for [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) during your semester abroad. This is only possible during the semester in which your stay abroad takes place, not in retrospect. - I have dual citizenship. Which tuition fee do I have to pay? You can only enter one citizenship in u:space. You have one citizenship from an EU/EAA country or Switzerland and another from a non-EU/EEA country? Enter the citizenship that is more beneficial for you. If your citizenship has changed, please message us for a [change of personal data](/en/change-of-personal-data/). - I study at two public universities in Austria. Where do I have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee? You can choose where you pay. The fee will be forwarded to all universities you are admitted to via the Austrian Federal Computer Center ("Bundesrechenzentrum"). If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges that will be deducted from your account! → Solution: Pay the fee again. Make sure to enter the correct data in the correct field. 2. You have paid a too high/too little fee. If you have paid too much (double payment, wrong prescribed fee) we can reimburse the difference to the required fee. → Solution: Submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). If you have paid to little you can pay the remaining difference until the end of the payment period. Make sure to enter the correct payment data. If the fee is not paid fully or in due time your degree programme will be closed. → Solution: Pay the remaining difference before the end of the payment period. 3. You have entered the unique payment reference (called "Semesterkundendaten" before) of a wrong semester If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: / - How do I know, how much I have to pay? You can see how much you have to pay in the current semester in u:space at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship. There are also differences between degree programmes and non-degree programmes. - [Detailed info about the amount of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) - Where can I check, if I have successfully paid or re-enroled in my degree programme? This is how you can check if your payment was successful and you have re-enroled in your studies at the University of Vienna: 1. You can find your student documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space at Personal matters > My documents. Choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents". 2. The status of the fee in u:space > Financial matters is "received"- 3. Choose your degree programme in u:space > Studies > Study overview. There you can see the following text with a green check mark: "Re-enrolled. You are re-enrolled for your degree programme for summer/winter semester 20xx (valid until xx.xx.xxxx)". - I am completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme

Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students federal body of Representatives](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en) - [Student’s Union of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Tolerance semesters](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31297&cHash=f0dee5ae0ea77af89fdfedaca07ca13b) - [Re-enrolment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31949&cHash=3e7137171e154715f00818454de75f06) - [Students’ Union (ÖH) insurance](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31953&cHash=ad6e71b25212dfc34b1314efc4ae3cb1) - [Payment period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32002&cHash=50174e9655aa9a11d1bb85ebc71fc50f) - Studienberechtigungsprüfung The Studienberechtigungsprüfung gives you the opportunity of admission to a degree programme if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. However, it is no general university entrance qualification. It is only valid for a certain group of degree programmes. Related Links: - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/) Related entries: - [General university entrance qualification](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31898&cHash=9a39467d6150693b5f0f6ba07ed49907) - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) The StudiesServiceCenter (in German: StudienServiceCenter, SSC) and the StudiesServiceUnit (in German: StudienServiceStelle, SSS) are central points of contact for questions about your degree programme. Each degree programme has a StudiesServiceCenter; the majority of degree programmes also have a StudiesServiceUnit, which informs you about your degree programme and the administration of your studies. On the websites of your SSC/SSS, you can find important information about your degree programme, relevant news and often forms to download. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You are not able to or do not want to pay the remaining difference? Please contact us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/706). We can remit the part of the fee you have already paid. - I am admitted to a continuing education programme at the Postgraduate Center and a bachelor/diploma or master programme. What do I have to keep in mind? If you are admitted at the Postgraduate and to a degree programme there are two options: 1. Only the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. No further payment or re-enrolment is necessary. You will be automatically re-enroled in your degree programme when the fee is received at the Postgraduate Center. 2. The tuition fee and the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you (due to your citizenship or because you have exceeded the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters) You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. In addition you have to pay the tuition fee (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union

reranked.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
reranked.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

After your application was approved, the same rules as for students with [citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland](#c1123190) apply to you. Keep the deadlines for your application for

heading_path: What do you need to consider?
reranked.text: Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the relevant authority whether you can receive family or student financial aid. - Insurance: Co-insurance with relatives or student self-insurance may not be possible. Please enquire directly with your insurance company.

reranked.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
reranked.text: / - I am EU/EEA citizen. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? Check [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Study overview to see which degree programmes you are admitted to. As soon as you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna all degree programmes are continued. It does not matter if you actively study them or not. The tuition fee is prescribed if you exceed the stipulated degree programme duration plus tolerance semesters. If a degree programme that you (currently) do not want to continue is in your study overview, [you can close it](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/). The amount of the tuition fee will then be calculated anew. - I have equal status. Why is the tuition fee prescribed for me? If you have equal status with EU/EEA citizens you are exempt from the tuition fee as long as - your residence permit is valid and - you are within the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters. [Detailed information about equal status with EU/EEA citizens](/en/tuition-fee/equal-status/) - Will my Erasmus+ semester be counted for the tuition-free period for EU/EEA citizens? Semesters during which you are abroad with a mobility programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would like to take a leave of absence. Is that possible? Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more

reranked.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
reranked.text: the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - Application for leave of absence granted After paying the tuition fee your [application for leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) was granted? A reimbursement is only possible if you have submitted your application for leave of absence before 30 November (for the winter semester) and 30 April (for the summer semester). - Application for remission granted Your application for remission of the tuition fee was granted after paying the tuition fee? The tuition fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too high You have paid a too high fee? The difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too little You have paid too little and are therefore no longer admitted at the University of Vienna? The fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - Reimbursement of the Students' Union fee The Students' Union fee does not remain at the University of Vienna. It is forwarded to the Students' Union. Therefore you have to submit your application for reimbursement [directly at the Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). The application is

reranked.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee

After your application was approved, the same rules as for students with [citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland](#c1123190) apply to you. Keep the deadlines for your application for equal status in mind! - Exemption 3: citizenship from the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland with admission to the degree programme before summer semester 2021 Students with this citizenship pay the same tuition fee as students with [citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland](#c1123190) if you - were admitted to the degree programme before 31 December 2020 and - do not interrupt the admission. If the degree programme is interrupted for one or more semesters, the same rules as for [students with non-EU/EEA citizenship](#c1123195) apply from the time of resumption of studies. - Exemption 4: Ukrainian citizens The University of Vienna automatically remits the tuition fee for winter semester 2026/27 for degree and non-degree students with Ukrainian citizenship. You only have to pay the Students' Union fee.

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period of the respective semester.

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

reranked.title: Amount of the tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
reranked.text: programme are counted in the same way as semesters you spend at the University of Vienna. You do not receive any additional tolerance semesters. As you pay the Students' Union fee during the Erasmus+ semester, you stay admitted at the University of Vienna. You therefore are also able to take examinations from courses at the University of Vienna that took place during your semester abroad (e.g. at a later examination date). If you have to pay the tuition fee, you can apply for [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) during your semester abroad. This is only possible during the semester in which your stay abroad takes place, not in retrospect. - I have dual citizenship. Which tuition fee do I have to pay? You can only enter one citizenship in u:space. You have one citizenship from an EU/EAA country or Switzerland and another from a non-EU/EEA country? Enter the citizenship that is more beneficial for you. If your citizenship has changed, please message us for a [change of personal data](/en/change-of-personal-data/). - I study at two public universities in Austria. Where do I have to pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee? You can choose where you pay. The fee will be forwarded to all universities you are admitted to via the Austrian Federal Computer Center ("Bundesrechenzentrum"). If you choose to pay at another university: Do not forget to re-enrol on your degree

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges that will be deducted from your account! → Solution: Pay the fee again. Make sure to enter the correct data in the correct field. 2. You have paid a too high/too little fee. If you have paid too much (double payment, wrong prescribed fee) we can reimburse the difference to the required fee. → Solution: Submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). If you have paid to little you can pay the remaining difference until the end of the payment period. Make sure to enter the correct payment data. If the fee is not paid fully or in due time your degree programme will be closed. → Solution: Pay the remaining difference before the end of the payment period. 3. You have entered the unique payment reference (called "Semesterkundendaten" before) of a wrong semester If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: / - How do I know, how much I have to pay? You can see how much you have to pay in the current semester in u:space at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship. There are also differences between degree programmes and non-degree programmes. - [Detailed info about the amount of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) - Where can I check, if I have successfully paid or re-enroled in my degree programme? This is how you can check if your payment was successful and you have re-enroled in your studies at the University of Vienna: 1. You can find your student documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space at Personal matters > My documents. Choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents". 2. The status of the fee in u:space > Financial matters is "received"- 3. Choose your degree programme in u:space > Studies > Study overview. There you can see the following text with a green check mark: "Re-enrolled. You are re-enrolled for your degree programme for summer/winter semester 20xx (valid until xx.xx.xxxx)". - I am completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme

You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. However, the leave of absence does not exempt you from paying the Student's Union fee. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? A [reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. heading_path: What do you need to consider?
selected.text: fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

selected.title: Leave of absence
selected.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

selected.title: Leave of absence
selected.text:
How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

Depending on the semester and the reason for your application, you must meet different deadlines:

- [Closing a degree programme](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/)
- [Resumption of a degree programme](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/)

[4] Leave of absence — FAQ
after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

[5] Leave of absence — Application
How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

Depending on the semester and the reason for your application, you must meet different deadlines:

[6] ABC of terminology — S
you have to pay the Student's Union fee every semester within the admission period, even if you are exempt from paying the tuition fee. The Student's Union (ÖH) fee does not remain at the university but is transferred to the Austrian National Union of Students. Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students federal body of Representatives](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en) - [Student’s Union of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Tolerance semesters](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31297&cHash=f0dee5ae0ea77af89fdfedaca07ca13b) - [Re-enrolment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31949&cHash=3e7137171e154715f00818454de75f06) - [Students’ Union (ÖH) insurance](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31953&cHash=ad6e71b25212dfc34b1314efc4ae3cb1) - [Payment period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32002&cHash=50174e9655aa9a11d1bb85ebc71fc50f) - Studienberechtigungsprüfung The Studienberechtigungsprüfung gives you the opportunity of admission to a degree programme if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. However, it is no general university entrance qualification. It is only valid for a certain group of degree programmes. Related Links: - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/) Related entries: - [General university entrance qualification](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31898&cHash=9a39467d6150693b5f0f6ba07ed49907) - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) The StudiesServiceCenter (in German: StudienServiceCenter, SSC) and the StudiesServiceUnit (in German: StudienServiceStelle, SSS) are central points of contact for questions about your degree programme. Each degree programme has a StudiesServiceCenter; the majority of degree programmes also have a StudiesServiceUnit, which informs you about your degree programme and the administration of your studies. On the websites of your SSC/SSS, you can find important information about your degree programme, relevant news and often forms to download. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Leave of absence — What do you need to consider?
absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending courses is - possible until the end of the [admission period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/) if you apply for a leave of absence during the admission period and were already admitted in the previous semester. - possible until the date of application if you apply for leave of absence after the end of the admission period. You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. However, the leave of absence does not exempt you from paying the Student's Union fee. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? A [reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester

[2] Leave of absence — What do you need to consider?
fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

Question: If I take a leave of absence, am I exempt from the tuition and Students' Union fees?

generate.raw_response: No, you are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence, but you are not exempt from paying the Students' Union fee [1][4][6].

answer: No, you are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence, but you are not exempt from paying the Students' Union fee [1][4][6].
